name: View Transitions — fundamentals
description: When to animate (communicate spatial continuity). `<ViewTransition>` wraps content; React coordinates `document.startViewTransition` — do not call it yourself. Triggers: enter/exit/update/share only with `startTransition`, `useDeferredValue`, or `Suspense`. Critical: VT must be before any DOM node (no wrapping `<div>` outside). Availability: without Next, use `react@canary`; Next App Router bundles canary — do not add canary just for VT. Browsers: Chromium 111+, Firefox 144+, Safari 18.2+ — degrade gracefully.

metadata.intent: Apply core View Transition rules without breaking enter/exit activation

must_use: Constrain VT to meaningful spatial/state continuity
conventions: Prefer `default="none"` and opt-in triggers explicitly per subtree.

steps.description: Ensure `<ViewTransition>` is the outermost wrapper for the animated subtree (first node in the fragment/tree), not nested under a parent `<div>` that appears before the VT.
Gate visual transitions on Concurrent features: wrap navigations/state flips that should animate in `startTransition`; pair Suspense boundaries for reveal animations.
